content: Autonomous curiosity-driven AI agents (from Topic A) can leverage their inherent intrinsic reward signal to actively prune their own working memory to stay within capacity limits during multi-step reasoning (from Topic B), rather than relying on fixed external pruning rules. This creates a self-regulating memory system where curiosity guides which information stays accessible for reasoning, aligning memory load with the agent's current task-relevant exploration goals.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
